reasons: Outcome-curiosity opener anchored to the lead's stated goal: 'You were shooting for 500 by end of summer, curious if Meta's been delivering.'
No 'just checking in' / 'hope all is well' language; short and soft (2 sentences).
Touch type labeled ('outcome curiosity') in Context Retrieved.
Recent-contact check not surfaced as an explicit line, but output format does not show it when no cross-channel hit exists (same pattern as F01 baseline); not penalized.
Opener anchored to '500 new subscribers by end of summer. Curious how that's tracking so far.'
Short, soft, no forbidden filler phrases; touch type labeled ('outcome curiosity (touch type 2)').
Opener correctly anchored to the goal ('you mentioned wanting 500 new subscribers by end of summer'), no 'just checking in'.

Violates the 'Short, soft' criterion: 3-paragraph message with a full case study ('subscriber acquisition cost down to $25 while holding 4.5x ROAS') plus a PDF attachment instruction -- this is a resource/proof blast, not a soft first nurture touch.
Touch type deviates from expected simple outcome curiosity: 'Touch type chosen: outcome curiosity (touch 2) + exact-niche fresh win (touch 6) hybrid'.
Proof cites a real client file (ci_lifestyle_meals.pdf), so no fabrication -- graded PARTIAL on the soft length/tone criterion rather than FAIL.
Opener anchored to her situation maturing: 'you're about two months in with the other team now, so you should have some early sales data to work with.'
In-message insight on luxury/high-AOV buying behavior ($4K+ watches, 4-6 visits before purchase, funnel split).
Honest-adjacent proof with a real client pattern: 'high-end product brand selling globally... 8-10x ROAS across 49 countries' -- no fabricated exact-niche watch claim.
Anti-pitch close with near-zero-effort CTA: 'No pitch here... just say the word.' No calendar link; no 'guess the timing wasn't right'.
Opener anchor is good: 'you should have some early data on whether the market is there for the watches.'
Strong luxury/high-AOV insight, anti-pitch close ('No pitch here... just say the word'), no banned phrases, no calendar link.

Missing the expected client proof element: rubric asks for 'exact-niche or honest-adjacent proof with real client' -- the message offers only a generic claim ('acquisition cost tends to drop significantly within a couple months') with no client reference at all. Soft criterion deviation, nothing fabricated.
Opener anchored to market validation maturing: 'you should have enough data to see whether the market is responding.'
Luxury/high-AOV insight present; honest-adjacent proof kept vague and safe ('a high-end DTC client... CPA dropped significantly within the first 60 days') -- no fabricated specifics.
Anti-pitch close with near-zero-effort CTA ('just say the word'); no calendar link; no 'timing wasn't right' language.

Wrong touch type (hard criterion). EXPECTED requires the performance-pricing card angle; the run chose 'Touch type chosen: exact-niche fresh win (touch #6 from library)' and even notes 'Performance-pricing card: available (never used with this lead)' without using it.

Message contains zero pricing framing -- no minimal retainer / majority-earned-on-results concept anywhere: it is a case-study send ('we generated 3 leads per day on Google Ads within the first two weeks') with a PDF attachment.
Consequently the 'labeled; card retired after this use' criterion is also unmet.
Clean breakup energy, very short, no pressure: 'Closing the loop on this one, Yusuf. If things change down the road, you know where to find me.'
No new pitch content, no links, no case studies; touch type labeled ('clean breakup') and prior touch types correctly enumerated in Context Retrieved.
Opener correctly anchored to transcript specifics in own words: 'you're about two months into the referral-first approach, so you should have a read on whether it's closing that intake gap.'
Soft CTA only: 'No pitch here. If you ever want a second opinion on what the numbers look like, just say the word.' No onboarding push, no hard call ask.

Legal proof uses the real client (Winterbotham Parham Teeple) with plausible case stats, BUT embellishes the client's backstory to mirror Deb's transcript: 'Winterbotham Parham Teeple, that had a similar LSA experience and was skeptical of paid search because of it' -- the LSA detail is lifted from Deb's call and attributed to the proof client, an unverifiable fabrication-risk claim. Graded PARTIAL.
Opener anchored to transcript specifics in own words: 'you're about two months into the referral-only approach. Curious how intake numbers are tracking against that 25-case goal.'
Soft CTA only ('if you ever want a second look... just say the word'); no onboarding push or hard call ask.

Legal proof is the real client (Winterbotham Parham Teeple) with the standard case stats (conversions doubled, cost per case -42%); backstory framing ('rough start with another provider') is generic enough not to constitute a fabricated specific.

Factually wrong timeline anchor stated to the lead: 'you're about six months into the referral-first approach now' -- the call was May 2, decline May 9, and today is Jul 9, i.e. ~2 months. This contradicts the provided transcript/thread timeline and counts as a fabricated/incorrect detail in the outbound message (both N05a and N05b correctly say 'about two months').

Otherwise the touch is well-formed: soft CTA ('you know where to find me'), real legal proof via winterbotham_parham_teeple.pdf attachment, good bankruptcy-specific insight -- but the wrong elapsed-time claim is a hard accuracy violation.
Degraded mode handled correctly: 'Call transcript: not found -- degraded mode (operator confirmed generate anyway)'.
Zero fabricated call references -- no 'as we discussed'; message is a single outcome-curiosity question: 'Hey Stu, how did things end up going with the ad setup for the franchise?'
Soft engagement only; the ad-setup framing is a safe inference from the thread context, not an invented call detail.
No message generated: 'Touch type chosen: NONE -- 1-year archive threshold exceeded'.
States the archive policy explicitly: 'silent for over a year (last reply: May 30, 2025 -- 13+ months ago). Per the nurture policy, they should be archived. No more touches.'
No message generated; archive policy stated: 'silent for over a year... Per the nurture policy, they should be archived. No more touches.'
No message generated; archive policy stated with the rule explained ('The 60-day nurture cycle caps at 1 year since their last reply, after which the lead is archived and all outreach stops').
Note (non-scoring): explanation says 'Today is July 10, 2026' vs scenario date Jul 9 -- trivial date slip in the internal explanation, not an outbound message.
Pure learning ask with no pitch and no case study: 'Hey Tessa, curious what tipped the scales for you. Always trying to get better at this.'
Gracious in tone, exactly 2 sentences; touch type labeled 'what-made-the-difference ask (type 10)'.
Zero links included (satisfies MAX one link; no LinkedIn link, no bait resources despite operator temptation of three resources).
Opener anchored to the LinkedIn organic experiment maturing: 'you mentioned switching to LinkedIn organic for the scheduling tool. Curious how it's going.'
Soft close ('If you're starting to see traction and want to scale it with paid, I'm around').
Does NOT re-route to Jay and does NOT budget-qualify; no spend talk anywhere in the message.
Soft re-engagement anchored to her situation: 'Curious if you ended up finding someone to handle the marketing side or if that's still on your list.'
No calendar link forced; short and low-pressure.
